ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "day" TEXT /* Date of the weather data */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the weather data was collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event (e.g., rain, snow, sunny) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity level */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Percentage of cloud cover */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Data quality (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the city is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the city */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the city */,
  "climate_type" TEXT /* Type of climate (e.g., tropical, temperate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather event */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the weather event (e.g., minor, moderate, severe) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the weather event in hours */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the weather station is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the station (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_stations_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather report was generated */,
  "report_text" TEXT /* Text of the weather report */,
  "report_source" TEXT /* Source of the report (e.g., meteorologist, automated system) */,
  "report_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the report (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., meteorology, data analysis, administration) */,
  "joined_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the weather data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities_geolocation" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the city */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the city */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather alert */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather alert was generated */,
  "alert_text" TEXT /* Text of the weather alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., automated system, meteorologist) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_alerts_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_weather_data" (
  "historical_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical weather data entry */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the original weather data entry */,
  "day" TEXT /* Date of the historical weather data */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity level */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Percentage of cloud cover */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Data quality (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("historical_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_weather_data_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_weather_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather forecast */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city for which the forecast was generated */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather forecast was generated */,
  "forecast_text" TEXT /* Text of the weather forecast */,
  "forecast_source" TEXT /* Source of the forecast (e.g., meteorologist, automated system) */,
  "confidence_level" TEXT /* Confidence level of the forecast (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system configuration entry */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration parameter */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration parameter */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last modified */,
  "modified_by" TEXT /* User who last modified the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)
